frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
generated_comment: I don’t see why people are getting so upset. A college is an educational institution, not a fashion parade, and some basic discipline in dress is perfectly reasonable, especially for girls of that age. In our society, decent presentation still matters, and when standards are removed, the overall culture of the campus also suffers. Freedom is not wrong, but it should come with maryada and responsibility.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment is misogynistic because it applies moral scrutiny specifically to young women's clothing and frames their appearance as tied to social decency and institutional order. It encodes a covert respectability-based policing frame: traditional dress is treated as respectable, while non-traditional dress is implied to be irresponsible or culturally harmful.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
